The paper was confirmed as existing by the automated checker (Semantic Scholar CorpusID:247079705). The automated checker flagged an author mismatch — citing two authors (Scott Mueller, Judea Pearl) when the database record lists only one (Scott Mueller). Web search results did not return a dedicated page for this paper. However, given that Judea Pearl is a well-known co-author frequently associated with Scott Mueller on causal inference work, and the paper is confirmed to exist, this is most likely a metadata incompleteness issue in the database record (Judea Pearl may have been omitted or listed differently), rather than a fabricated authorship. The paper itself is real and verified.

EXPLANATION: The paper exists and is verified in Semantic Scholar (CorpusID:247079705); Judea Pearl is a known collaborator of Scott Mueller on causal inference topics, and the author discrepancy is likely a database metadata omission rather than fabrication.
LINK: https://api.semanticscholar.org/CorpusID:247079705
FOUND_TITLE: Personalized decision making–a conceptual introduction
FOUND_AUTHORS: Scott Mueller
FOUND_YEAR: 2023
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
